fields.description:
## Why
Automate the FFO rebase-to-tip-then-submit so parallel agent sessions stop hand-rolling it and racing; serialize so the tested tree is always the merged tree with no thrash.

## What
A project service (Python, project infra â€” NOT `src/rebar` core) that, for the front `Autosubmit`+submittable change/chain: rebases to the current tip, awaits a fresh `Verified +1`, and ancestor-atomic submits the exact tested SHA. Holds a single in-flight `wipChain`.

## Dependencies
Depends on **S1** (the `Autosubmit` label + `autolander-bot` vote ACL): this loop selects on `label:Autosubmit+1`, so the label and its bot-only ACL must exist and be live before the loop can select or drive anything.

## Scope
Happy-path loop + serialization + identity/attribution. Failure paths, deploy, and the agent-facing command are separate stories.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Selects the front `label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable` change/chain; **FIFO ordering keyed on the `Autosubmit` vote's timestamp** (oldest-voted-submittable-first).
- [ ] **Rebase-selection rule:** if the change heads a relation chain with >1 member (detected via `GET /changes/{id}?o=CURRENT_REVISION` + the related-changes / `SUBMITTED_TOGETHER` set), use `POST /rebase:chain`; a single change or a `--no-ff` merge change uses `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` (first-parent-only â†’ `MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE`). A merge commit that is *also* a chain tip is treated as a chain. `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` preserves author/DCO `Signed-off-by`/`rebar-ticket` trailer.
- [ ] Awaits a **fresh** `Verified +1` â€” defined as a `Verified +1` on the exact rebased patchset SHA (not a copied/carried vote from a prior patchset) â€” before submit (`LLM-Review` carries `TRIVIAL_REBASE`/`MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE` per ADR-0041; `Verified` re-runs on the new patchset).
- [ ] Ancestor-atomic submit of the exact tested SHA. **FFO TOCTOU guard:** immediately before `POST /submit`, re-verify `is:submittable` **on that tested SHA**; if `main` advanced or the SHA changed, do NOT submit â€” re-drive rebase.
- [ ] **`wipChain` schema:** a single in-memory record `{change_id, chain_member_ids[], tested_sha, verified_at, phase}` held by the single instance; `tested_sha` is updated to the post-rebase SHA before awaiting Verified.
- [ ] Single `wipChain` / single instance (serialized) â€” never rebases multiple changes onto the same HEAD concurrently.
- [ ] Reads existing `Verified` votes only; casts NO votes (no gate-semantics change in Phase 1).
